repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell University Department of Music records
did.physdesc: Correspondence, printed materials, financial records, photographs and drawings.
did.abstract: Correspondence and financial records; programs for University Orchestra concerts, recitals, and music festivals; scores of student productions; drawings for a proposed Department of Music building; historical sketches of the University Orchestra and photographs.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence pertaining to the Library Tower chimes; programs for University Orchestra concerts, recitals, and music festivals; scores of student productions; drawings for a proposed Department of Music building; historical sketches of the University Orchestra; many photographs concerning the activities of the Department. Also, records pertaining to the Festival of Contemporary Arts, financial records of the Department, and correspondence of Department chair Paul J. Weaver concerning music education, courses, students and faculty.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cornell University Department of Music records, #14-20-513. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: College Students -- New York (State) -- Ithaca -- Societies and clubs.
Music -- Instruction and study -- New York (State) -- Ithaca.
controlaccess.corpname: Cornell University. Festival of Contemporary Arts

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

did.unittitle: Johnston, Cyril F. - Chimes (Gillett & Johnston Firm)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two Copies of 1 letter from Johnston to Weaver re: purchase of a new bell and problems with overseas transportation & Shipping due to World War II

did.unittitle: Ackerman, Frederick L. - Music Dept. Proposals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One document, 8 p. entitled "Memorandum of Conferences with Mr. Worcester". Discusses proposed revisions in music curriculum/major and a proposed School of Fine Arts at Cornell, including discussion of plans for a new "physical facility"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: P. J. Weaver Correspondence to Chimes Advisory Committee, including details of the organization of the Committee. (1933-1934) "Misplaced documents: 6 Booklets in envelope - Lee, Richard H. ("41) - Chimes and campus; including arranging music for chimes, planning programs, playing the chimes and other suggestions. Ithaca, Cayuga Press, May 1943. And letter from W. Worcester (Amsterdam architect firm) to P. J. W. re: proposed music building (see item # 2)

did.unittitle: Chimes - Meigs (R. B. Meigs, Morrill Hall)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Weaver to Meigs re: Bassett chimes and their installation.

did.unittitle: Chimes - Lefevre (Mr. Kamiel Lefevre, carillonneur of Riverside Church, New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with Weaver re: playing mechanisms for chimes, with references to Gillette and Johnston firm and meeting of the Congress of North American Guild of Carillonneurs at Riverside Church. Includes: Booklet describing Riverside Church and Pamphlet describing Riverside Church.

did.unittitle: Chimes - Gillett & Johnston (Bell founders, Croydon, England)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence about a new bell for Cornell and proposals for work on recasting, tuning and preserving inscriptions of old bells. Includes: Booklet about G & J bells; Brochure about G & J Bells; Photo of Wichita, Kansas bells made by G & J.

did.unittitle: Chimes - Meneely (Alfred C. Meneely; Meneely & Co.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between Meneely and Weaver re: new bells, recasting old bells, electric carillon; includes references to old A. D. White/Meneely correspondence. (see # 11). Diagrams of Meneely Co. bells and console system included. Includes: Letter from Weaver to Dayton C. Miller, Case School of Applied Science, Cleveland - re: University of Rochester bells made by Meneely.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between Rogalsky and Weaver re: ordering, payment of installation of new bells.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with Weaver re: installing carillon, new playing mechanism, recasting old bells. Correspondence with Weaver re: repair work needed on one of two bells recently installed by Meneely. Misplaced documents: Letter to rev. Reinhold Niebuhr, Union Theological Seminary, about music to be performed in Sage Chapel during his visit to Cornell. Correspondence between Weaver and George A. Wedge, Dean of Julliard School of Music re: Mr. Sun's enrollment at Julliard.

did.unittitle: van Bergen, H. T. - Netherland Pavilion (Bell founder; in U. S. during World's Fair)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence with Weaver re: purchasing/revamping carillon.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed excerpts from other documents - Includes: Excerpts of letters from Meneely Co. to President White and Treasurer E. L. Williams about Chimes. Excerpts from trustee minutes about chimes.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Schedule of prices for chimes ringing (memo from Netschert to Weaver ). Penciled notes on carillons heard outside of Ithaca.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between Peters and Weaver re: installation of 2 new bells and acquiring an electric chimer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hugh E. Weatherlow, superintendent of buildings and grounds dept; John A. Peters & H. R. Morgan, buildings & grounds dept.) Correspondence with "Weaver about installation costs. Estimate worksheets for installation.

did.unittitle: Chimes--Durham - (Prof. C. L. Durham (Goldwin Smith Hall)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of 1 letter to Durham from Weaver describing chimes and problems of new playing mechanism, with mention of Weaver's upcoming trip to Colgate Rochester Divinity School . (see # 21).

did.unittitle: Deagan, D. C. Inc. · Inc.--Chicago, Illinois (bell founders)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between Jack Deagan and Weaver about adding/altering chimes. Includes: Booklet: "Deagan tower chimes; electrically operated. J.C. Deagan, Inc." and Telegram from Deagan to Weaver about visit to Florida World's Fair Pavilion.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Weaver about Jack C. Deagan's interest in Cornell chimes (signed by P. K. Neuses). Contains a Pamphlet: "Interesting facts about the world's largest carillon; Florida Building, New York World's Fair"

did.unittitle: Smith, Albert W., editor, (class of 1873) - "The bells of Cornell"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: History Cornell chimes. Description of chimes. Includes words to songs played on chimes. Includes lists of chimemasters from '71 - '30, with '31 penciled in. 48p. with illustrations and photos. Also, newspaper clipping pasted in end of book. (from Oswego, NY). Clipping discusses a "new manuscript book" which includes 402 numbers arranged by chimemasters during the last 10 years that was presented to the University Library. Signed by Paul Hulslander, '31, chimemaster in 1931.

did.unittitle: Riegger, Harold Eaton, chimemaster 1909-1913 - "Bells and Chimes"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With particular reference to those at Cornell University. S.1. : s.p., 1913. History of Cornell Chimes. Includes inscriptions on the bells and lists of chimemasters through 1913. 20p. - Photos. Signed by Paul Hulslander, '31, chimemaster in 1931.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A brief history of the bells; together with some description and the programs of the concerts at their rededication, Ithaca: Andrus & Church. Includes inscriptions on the bells. Concerts played by H. G. Bull '08. 15 p. Photos

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Mary Roberts Coolidge (class of 1882) to Weaver, with Weaver reply: "first woman to have played the Cornell Chimes somewhere about 1880"

did.unittitle: President Day to P. J. W.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Committee on Chimes - Letter from President Day to P. J. W. re: chairing a "Committee to make a thorough going study of all phases of the matter". Committee members proposed: Charles K. Bassett, Mrs. Anna Gauntlet Whitcomb, Dean A. W. Smith, Prof. Eugene P. Andrews. Memo (from "her" to P. J. W.) reporting A. W. Smith message about Committee.

did.unittitle: Chimes - Bassett - Donation by Bassett of New Bells

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding donation of money by Charles K. Bassett for 2 new bells. 3 copies of press release (typescript) about Bassett's gift & brief history of chimes. Copies of President Day - Bassett correspondence about serving on chimes committee. Correspondence between Day and Weaver re: Bassett's membership on committee. Copy of J. B. Trousdale (auditor) letter to Bassett acknowledging donation of money for installation of bells. Misplaced documents: Letter from R. B. Meigs (Asst. Deputy Comptroller) to Weaver re: installation of new bells. (see # 4). Letter from Colgate Rochester Divinity School to Weaver re: visit to their chimes. (see # 15)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence between Paul Weaver and Lee Smith re: editing, proofs of songbook; 1940 (see also #24 - Fischer correspondence)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chiefly correspondence between Weaver and von der Goltz - re: copyright of songs; 1940. Correspondence with Gustav Reese (G. Schirmer Inc.) re: publication rights (see also #24 - Fischer correspondence and #25 - 0ct.-Nov. 1940)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Alphabetical Correspondence File - 1932 - 1943. Chiefly correspondence to/from Paul J. Weaver. Includes Minutes of First meeting of "Song Book Committee". List of songs to be considered for inclusion/omission. Section clipped together labelled "Ashbery" (Ray Ashbery, Cornell Alumni Office). Ashbery/Weaver correspondence. Ashbery/Connor (Carl Fisher, Inc.) correspondence (see also document item # 26. These documents do not duplicate material in # 25 and # 26. Correspondence tracking composers/history of individual Cornell songs. Correspondence regarding copyright permission for Cornell songs. Section of Fischer correspondence re: copyright, contracts, editing. Questionnaire sent to various persons re: title of songbook, inclusions, pictures. Correspondence from alumni about songs to include. Correspondence to/from Creed W. Fulton, President of Cornell Alumni Assoc. Non songbook material includes: (see item # 2). 3 letters with Ackerman (architectural firm) about proposed music facility (1929)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Chronological Correspondence file chiefly between Ray Ashbery and Paul Weaver. Includes copies of letters of correspondence between Ashbery and others. Includes correspondence with Thornton W. Allen Co. about Cornell songbook publications, and with Carl Fischer Co./ Frank Connor and Intercollegiate Music League. Copyright and publication rights for Cornell songs, with reference to earlier editions of the song-book published by Lent and by Hinds, Haydon & Eldridge. References to George Louis Coleman, S. H. (Hibby) Ayer. Non songbook material includes: Weaver memo re: ringing chimes for '37 commencement.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Copies of Ashbery-Connor correspondence re: contract, copyright, Allan Co, IMC; some reference to documents in #24 - 25. Non song book material includes: Program of music dept. concert, May 5, 1934 - "Elijah"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Cornell Masque Scores

did.unittitle: Panatela - A political comic opera in three acts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written for the Cornell Masque. Book and lyrics by Kenneth L. Roberts ('08), Romeyn Berry ('04). Music by T. J. Lindorff ('07), H. C. Schuyler ('10), H. E. Childs ('09). s.1.: The Cornell Masque, cl907. printed score (54p.)

did.unittitle: Popocaterpillar VII - An Aztec comedy opera in three acts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written for the Cornell Masque. Book by H.J. Snyder ('08), H. L. Dawson ('07). Music by G. F. Pond ('10), H. E. Childs ('10), T. J. Lindorff ('07), H. C. Schuyler ('10), C. Previn('l0). s.1.: The Cornell Masque, 1908. "Published by Arthur W. Tams Music Library, New York City." printed score (84p.)

did.unittitle: Oolong: A Chinese comedy opera in two acts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written for the Cornell Masque (revised and enlarged from the president of Oolong). By Romeyn Berry ('04). Music by Charles Previn ('09), R. K. Blanchard ('10), H. F. Welch ('10), G. C. May ('11), W. L. Wood. s .1. : The Cornell Masque, cl909. "Published by A. W. Tams Music Library, New York" printed score (66p.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: C.U. Orchestra Concerts and Music Department Concerts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concert given at home of Mr. & Mrs. Paul J. Weaver, Ithaca, N.Y.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Additional Program received Aug. 29, 1961

did.unittitle: Series II. Musical Clubs - George Coleman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Additional Index terms of collection

did.unittitle: deForest, Henry P. (Librarian, Cornell Club of New York City)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Coleman about Cornell Songs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typed excerpts - 2 copies - of music-related items in the Era

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Article on Dann in Cornell in Era

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Which is in the "letters" folder in the Manuscripts, dated folder.

did.unittitle: Clippings from N.Y. Times and Harvard Alumni Bulletin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Separated from letters in which they were originally enclosed.

did.unittitle: Songs of Cornell Copyright and ISBN info.
Songs of Cornell: Class of 1957 Glee Club Fund
Songs of Cornell: Class of 1957 Glee Club Funds
Songs of Cornell: Class of 1957 Glee Club Funds
Studies of the A. B. "Rossiter Committee
College of Arts and Sciences Memoranda re: Music Dept.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Featuring Johannes Brahms, Karel Husa (Conductor), and Thomas Sokol (Director)
